title: Fluffy Norwegian Rice Noodle Stir-Fry
description: A light, umami-rich stir-fry that blends rice stick noodles with Nordic flavors for a cozy, low-calorie lunch.
instructions: Cook the rice stick noodles in salted boiling water for 3-4 minutes until al dente, then drain and rinse under cold water. Set aside.
Whisk together low-sodium soy sauce, miso paste, and reindeer broth until the miso is fully dissolved. Set the sauce aside.
Heat a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat and add sesame oil.
Add the julienned carrot and snow peas to the pan, stir-frying for 2 minutes until they reach a tender-crisp texture.
If using the egg, push the veggies to the side, crack the egg into the pan, and scramble until just set; then mix it with the veggies.
Add the drained noodles to the pan and pour the sauce mixture over them. Toss gently to coat, then cook for 1-2 minutes until the noodles are heated through and the sauce has slightly thickened.
Stir in the chopped dill, sliced scallions, and a pinch of salt and pepper. Toss again for 30 seconds to combine flavours.
Remove from heat and serve immediately, garnishing with extra dill if desired.
